feature: bober-code-reviewer agent + bober.code-review skill + orchestrator wiring
description: Add a new fresh-context subagent (bober-code-reviewer) that runs after the evaluator passes a sprint. Performs code-quality review separate from rubric-based evaluation. Writes an advisory report to .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md. Output is advisory only — critical findings surface in the run-summary but do NOT block sprint completion in this spec. Requires a localized orchestrator change to spawn the new agent at one decision point.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter (name: bober-code-reviewer, description, tools list, model). Tools list contains Read, Bash, Grep, Glob ONLY — no Write, no Edit, no MultiEdit, no NotebookEdit (read-only constraint mirroring bober-evaluator).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read agents/bober-code-reviewer.md. Verify frontmatter parses as YAML. Verify the tools field contains only the four read-only tools.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md contains a 'Subagent Context' block matching the pattern in bober-evaluator.md (explanation of isolated context, instruction to read contract from disk, structured JSON response contract). The structured response contract documents a ReviewResult JSON shape with: reviewId, contractId, specId, timestamp, summary, critical (array), important (array), minor (array), approvedAreas (array).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read agents/bober-code-reviewer.md. Locate the Subagent Context block. Locate the ReviewResult JSON schema. Verify the four issue-severity arrays.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md contains the Iron Law block adopted in Sprint 5 style: 'NO REVIEW WITHOUT FILE-PATH-AND-LINE-NUMBER EVIDENCE FOR EVERY FINDING' (or equivalent), plus Red Flags and Rationalization-Prevention table (>=5 entries each), consistent with the other agent prompts.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep agents/bober-code-reviewer.md for the Iron Law, Red Flags section, and Rationalization-Prevention table. Verify presence and entry counts.

successCriteria.description: skills/bober.code-review/SKILL.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter (name: bober-code-review, description starts with 'Use when completing a sprint' or similar trigger). Body contains: (a) what to check (DRY violations, YAGNI violations, dead code, missing tests, surprising patterns, anti-pattern citations from .bober/anti-patterns/), (b) what NOT to flag (style preferences, opinions on naming when consistent with codebase, theoretical risks), (c) a Process Flow section documenting the run order.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read skills/bober.code-review/SKILL.md. Verify frontmatter. Locate the three required content blocks.

successCriteria.description: src/orchestrator/ has a localized change: after the evaluator returns overallResult='pass' for a sprint, the orchestrator spawns bober-code-reviewer with the contract and the eval result; the spawn happens BEFORE the sprint is marked complete. If the code reviewer crashes or times out, the orchestrator logs a warning and proceeds (advisory only).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep src/orchestrator/ for 'bober-code-reviewer' or 'code-reviewer'. Read the surrounding code path. Verify it triggers on evaluator pass, has error handling for reviewer failure (does not block on failure).

successCriteria.description: When the code reviewer runs successfully, it produces .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md. The file follows a documented schema: markdown with sections '# Code Review: <contractId>', '## Summary', '## Critical', '## Important', '## Minor', '## Approved Areas'. At minimum the Summary section is populated; the four severity sections may be empty if no findings.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: After running a sprint end-to-end (mocked or real), verify the file exists at the expected path with the documented sections. Alternatively, verify the orchestrator code path writes a file matching this schema and the writer is unit-tested.

successCriteria.description: Unit test coverage: at least one new test exercises the orchestrator's code-reviewer spawn path. The test mocks the evaluator returning overallResult='pass' and asserts: (a) the code-reviewer is spawned with the correct contract and eval-result inputs, (b) the resulting review file is written to the expected path, (c) if the code-reviewer errors, the sprint is still marked complete (advisory behavior).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Locate the new test file. Read it. Verify the three assertions are exercised. Run `npm run test` and confirm the new test passes.

successCriteria.description: If a Critical finding is reported by the code reviewer, the orchestrator surfaces it in the run-summary output (visible to the user) but does NOT block sprint completion or trigger a generator retry. This is the advisory-only contract.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the orchestrator code path. Verify the run-summary builder includes critical findings. Verify there is NO control-flow branch from 'critical findings present' to 'retry generator' or 'block sprint'.

successCriteria.description: All existing eval strategies pass on the agent-bober repo (typecheck, lint, build, test). No regression from the orchestrator change.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Run `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test` — assert exit 0.

successCriteria.description: The new orchestrator code does not modify the existing evaluator code path (bober-evaluator behavior is unchanged). Code-reviewer is an addition, not a refactor.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: git diff src/orchestrator/ on the affected files. Verify the diff is additive within the evaluator-pass branch — no deletions or modifications to evaluator-call logic itself.

expectedChanges.description: New read-only subagent for code-quality review after evaluator pass
Skill documenting when/what/how of the code-review pass
Add code-reviewer spawn after evaluator pass; localized change at one decision point
New unit test exercising the spawn path and advisory-only behavior
Directory for per-sprint review markdown files (created at first run)

generatorNotes:
This is the highest-risk sprint of the spec — it's the only one with orchestrator code changes. KEEP THE CHANGE LOCALIZED: find the single decision point where the orchestrator handles evaluator overallResult='pass' (likely in src/orchestrator/ — explore with grep for 'overallResult' or 'EvalResult'). Add ONE call site for the code-reviewer there. Do NOT refactor surrounding code, do NOT extract abstractions, do NOT add error-handling beyond what's needed for advisory-mode (catch reviewer crash → log warning → proceed).

References for the new agent: /tmp/superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md (the prompt template), agents/bober-evaluator.md (the read-only subagent pattern in bober). The bober-code-reviewer should:
- Read the sprint contract from .bober/contracts/<contractId>.json
- Read the eval result from .bober/eval-results/<evalId>.json
- Read the diff produced by the sprint (via git diff against HEAD~N or whatever the contract's base ref is)
- Run grep/glob to verify each finding has file:line evidence
- Emit a ReviewResult JSON to its return value AND write a markdown file to .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md

The ReviewResult JSON shape (parsed by orchestrator):
```
{
  reviewId: 'review-<contractId>-<timestamp>',
  contractId: string,
  specId: string,
  timestamp: ISO-8601,
  summary: string (2-3 sentences),
  critical: Array<{ description: string, evidence: Array<{ path, line, snippet }> }>,
  important: Array<same shape>,
  minor: Array<same shape>,
  approvedAreas: Array<string>
}
```

For the skill, write a focused 100-150 line SKILL.md. The 'what to check' list should cite anti-pattern names from .bober/anti-patterns/ (created in Sprint 4) — for example: 'Reference .bober/anti-patterns/testing-anti-patterns.md when reviewing test changes'. The 'what NOT to flag' list is critical to prevent the reviewer from becoming a bikeshed generator — explicitly forbid style preferences, naming opinions when consistent, theoretical risks without evidence, and disagreements with already-resolved spec clarifications.

Orchestrator integration: be conservative. The new code path must:
1. Trigger ONLY on evaluator overallResult='pass'
2. Use the same subagent-spawn mechanism the orchestrator already uses for evaluator (Look for the existing pattern; do not invent a new one)
3. Time-box the reviewer (suggested: 5 minutes — make this configurable via bober.config.json if a config knob already exists, otherwise hard-code with a TODO)
4. On reviewer error/timeout: log to console.error, write a stub review file noting the failure, continue to mark sprint complete
5. On reviewer success: parse the ReviewResult JSON, validate it has the expected shape, write the markdown file, attach critical findings (if any) to the orchestrator's run summary

Unit test: place in the existing tests/ directory following the existing test pattern (vitest or jest — check package.json). Mock the subagent spawn. Assert the three contract points (spawn happens, file is written, advisory-only on failure).

DO NOT modify bober-evaluator.md in this sprint — Sprint 4 and Sprint 5 already covered evaluator changes. Touching it again here would conflict.
evaluatorNotes:
This sprint has the highest regression risk because it's the only one with orchestrator code changes. Run the FULL eval strategy suite (typecheck, lint, build, test) and verify exit 0 on each. Verify the new test actually exercises the spawn path — a test that passes by mocking everything including the assertion target is not real coverage.

Verify the change is LOCALIZED: the diff for src/orchestrator/ should be small (<150 lines of additions, mostly new code, minimal modifications to existing code). If the generator refactored the evaluator-pass handler, flag as scope creep.

Verify the advisory-only contract: the orchestrator must NOT have a code path where critical findings trigger generator retry or sprint failure. This is the agreed scope for this spec — a future spec may upgrade code reviewer to blocking. Reviewer's role in THIS spec is observability.

Verify the new agent's tools field actually restricts to read-only. A bober-code-reviewer with Write tools is a contract violation that would not be caught at runtime.

Verify .bober/reviews/ directory creation is idempotent (mkdir -p semantics).

Verify the integration with Sprint 4's anti-pattern catalog: the skill body cites .bober/anti-patterns/ by path, and the reviewer's prompt includes the same reference. If Sprint 4 deferred or the catalog is missing, flag the dependency violation.

Check git history: this sprint must build on Sprint 7's state without disturbing earlier sprint outputs. If any of the Iron Law blocks, Red Flags tables, AGENTS.md, anti-pattern docs, or hooks added by earlier sprints have been modified by this sprint's generator, that's a regression — those should be untouched.
